title: docs: clarify idmap mount option syntax
body:
Fixes: #24249

Removed the incorrect description that the `idmap` option accepts `true` or `false`, added the correct descriptions, and included relevant examples.

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:

commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)

- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

comments.body:
> This is a quick-fix for the incorrect documentation; long-term, @eriksjolund's coverage table idea as proposed in the linked issue sounds like a good way to avoid documentation related drifts in the future.

I agree it is still valuable. Let's do that in this PR before we forget about it.

> Let's do that in this PR before we forget about it.

Sorry @Honny1; I missed this. I suggested that we could get this in quickly since the documentation is clearly incorrect, and the table approach might take some time to land given the scope.
That is okay; you kept open that issue. Thanks
